role_delineation_check: The Social Worker manages the clinical referral process and agency selection, while the Navigator audits demographic data in Atlantis, provides program education to the family, and flags any reported service gaps to the facility team without contacting outside vendors.
narrative_summary: Arthur, a 70-year-old patient recovering from spinal fusion surgery, was prepared for a Friday discharge to his home with his son's support. Despite the Social Worker's confirmation of a home health agency, the assigned nurse failed to appear for the initial assessment on Saturday morning, causing significant distress for the family. The Navigator identified this critical gap during a follow-up interaction, immediately documented the no-show in Atlantis, and facilitated a direct connection between the family and the Social Worker to resolve the staffing failure. By acting as a liaison, the Navigator ensured that the facility team was alerted to the failure while maintaining the proper boundaries of the coordination role.
demographic_audit_note: The Navigator audited the patient face sheet against the Atlantis record and discovered that Arthur's primary phone number was listed with an incorrect area code. This was corrected in the Atlantis database to ensure that the home health team and the Medical Assistant could reach him on the day of discharge.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with skilled nursing and physical therapy
v_card_flyer_status: The Navigator met with Arthur at his bedside and provided the program flyer, explaining how the Medical Assistant supports the transition home. The Navigator then assisted Arthur in saving the digital V-Card into his phone contacts so he would recognize the caller ID for his upcoming appointments.
pre_dc_pulse_call: The Navigator conducted the 24-hour pulse call with Arthur's son, who confirmed that the home was prepared but expressed concern about the specific arrival time of the nursing staff, which the Navigator deferred to the Social Worker.
atlantis_final_sync: The Navigator recorded the discharge date of Friday the 12th and confirmed the Medical Assistant's first visit was scheduled for Saturday morning in the Atlantis log.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Navigator met with Arthur to discuss the Healing Partners program; Arthur expressed moderate anxiety about managing his mobility after his spinal fusion.
The Social Worker confirmed that the home health agency had accepted the referral; the Navigator documented the agency name and updated the wait status in Atlantis.

The Navigator conducted the 24-hour pulse call and learned the family had not yet heard from the agency regarding a visit window; the Navigator advised the son to ask the Social Worker for the specific schedule.
Arthur was discharged to his home; the Navigator finalized the Atlantis entry for the transition and scheduled the Medical Assistant for the following morning.

The family contacted the Navigator to report that the home health nurse had not arrived for the scheduled visit; the Navigator documented the gap in Atlantis and instructed the family to contact the Social Worker immediately.

format_2_triples.situation: Arthur's son was confused about why the Medical Assistant was coming if a nurse was already assigned.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Clarified the distinct role of the Healing Partners program and the Medical Assistant in supporting the first 24 hours of the transition.

format_2_triples.situation: The Social Worker had not yet shared the name of the chosen home health agency for the spinal fusion recovery.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Requested a verbal update from the Social Worker regarding the referral status to ensure the Atlantis record was accurate.

format_2_triples.situation: The family reported that the home health nurse failed to show up for the Saturday morning appointment.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Logged the reported service failure in Atlantis and advised the family to reach out to the Social Worker for clinical coordination.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator notices the home health agency has not confirmed a start-of-care time but the Social Worker is currently in a care conference; the Navigator chooses to wait for the Social Worker’s update and documents the waiting period in Atlantis.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the Social Worker's ownership of the discharge timeline and prevents the Navigator from contacting vendors directly.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Upon hearing from the family that the nurse did not arrive as scheduled, the Navigator documents the specific details in Atlantis and asks the Social Worker for a status update to ensure the patient's transition remains safe.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This fulfills the role of a liaison by keeping the facility team informed while ensuring the family handles the clinical communication with the Social Worker.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator calls the home health agency's intake department directly to demand an explanation for the missed visit and attempts to coordinate a new arrival time for the nurse on the family's behalf.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the no-vendor rule and oversteps into clinical coordination, which is the sole responsibility of the Social Worker.
